steps.title: Project goal analysis
steps.objective: Clarify the goal, scope, constraints, acceptance criteria, and highest-risk unknowns.
steps.handoff: Pass a brief with scope, acceptance criteria, risks, and open assumptions in 5 bullets or fewer.

steps.title: System architecture planning
steps.objective: Turn the goal brief into a practical architecture, implementation shape, and interface plan.
steps.handoff: Pass component boundaries, data flow notes, file-level targets, and trade-offs in concise bullets.

steps.title: Task breakdown
steps.objective: Break the architecture into a low-risk execution order suitable for a small local model workflow.
steps.handoff: Pass a numbered implementation checklist with the minimum required sequence and success criteria.

steps.title: Code implementation
steps.objective: Implement the next thin slice of work with minimal context and explicit file changes.
steps.handoff: Pass changed files, any unresolved issues, and the exact review focus for the auditor.

steps.title: Code review
steps.objective: Review the implementation for correctness, edge cases, maintainability, and spec alignment.
steps.handoff: Pass defects, required fixes, and QA focus areas only.

steps.title: QA testing
steps.objective: Verify expected behavior, test coverage, and regression risk with concise validation notes.
steps.handoff: Pass verified scenarios, failed scenarios, and doc updates needed by the documentation writer.

steps.title: Documentation generation
steps.objective: Produce the final implementation summary, usage notes, and change log for the completed slice.
steps.handoff: Pass a short release-ready summary or next-step note.
